Hero.desc: A clear, interactive guide to voluntary carbon market standards. Learn how strict international requirements convert into safety and profit for your farm.

About.title: Why is the process so strict?
About.desc: Approval from global verifiers requires attention to hundreds of details. We take all the complex expertise upon ourselves, leaving you with full operational transparency.
About.card1Desc: We take responsibility for full compliance of your project with the complex methodologies of international standards (Verra, Gold Standard).
About.card2Desc: We organize the collection of required data necessary to justify the baseline and confirm project additionality.
About.card3Desc: Global investors only pay for verified units. Our process guarantees that your work will be confirmed by audit.

Timeline.title: Project Lifecycle
Timeline.desc: Interactive map: your path from initial idea to the issuance of the first carbon credits.
Timeline.step1Desc: Land potential assessment. We conduct a preliminary audit, select optimal methodology and standards (ARR, SOC).
Timeline.step2Desc: A multi-page project justification for the registry: why the project is important and what its baseline is.
Timeline.step3Desc: Independent international audit of project documentation before the project starts.
Timeline.step3Tag1: Shared task: Responding to auditor requests
Timeline.step4Desc: Using satellite imagery (dMRV), sensors, and reports to confirm forest growth and soil improvements.

Academy.desc: Key principles that you will often hear about during our collaboration.

Calculator.heroDesc: Estimate the approximate number of carbon credits (tCO₂e) for your agroforestry project. Modeling is performed using IPCC (2006) methodology.
Calculator.speciesWarning: ⚠ Please choose at least 5 species. (This increases forest resilience and increases sequestration).
Calculator.calcNote: Modeling is based on IPCC (2006) biomass growth coefficients. Data is indicative for decision-making. Accurate calculation is possible only after PDD development.
Calculator.placeholderText: Enter your site parameters on the left and click "Calculate Online"
Calculator.methodDesc: Calculation is based on IPCC 2006 (moderate/arid climate). Conversion: C → CO₂e × 3.667. Only above-ground tree biomass is included. Reduction coefficients applied for types: agroforestry (×0.62) and windbreaks (×0.55). Species diversity (≥5) positively affects the calculation.
Calculator.incomeDesc: Estimated revenue based on different carbon credit price scenarios in the voluntary carbon market (VCM).
Calculator.incomeExplanation: * For NbS (Nature-based Solutions) projects with high social impact and contribution to Sustainable Development Goals (SDG), VCM pricing is significantly higher than the market average. Such projects (including agroforestry and drought-resistant planting) are traded with a 'quality premium', as corporate buyers seek to minimize reputational risks.

Standards.heroDesc: Original methodologies (PDF) of leading international registries and their brief summary to plain language for our partners.
Standards.emptyFilter: Standards for this registry haven't been added yet.
Standards.howToDesc: Original English-language PDF documents are primarily intended for our engineers and auditors. For your convenience, we have prepared brief summaries via the
